enrichment.short_description: Temporal is an open-source durable execution platform that lets developers write reliable workflows as code with automatic state persistence and retries.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Temporal is a durable execution platform for building reliable workflows in code, with automatic retries, state persistence, and full replay visibility.

features.title: Durable Workflows
features.description: Business logic is written as code and executed as a Temporal Workflow with automatic state persistence.

features.title: Activities with automatic retries
features.description: Functions that retry automatically and recover seamlessly from failures.

features.title: Built-in state management
features.description: The Temporal Service persists application state and provides built-in retries, task queues, signals, and timers.

features.title: Full execution visibility
features.description: Tracks workflow execution history without requiring extensive log analysis.

features.title: Replay and recovery
features.description: Workflows can be recovered, replayed, or paused at any point in execution.

features.title: Multi-language SDKs
features.description: Supports Python, Go, TypeScript, Ruby, C#, Java, and PHP.

pros: Open-source with a self-hosted option in addition to managed cloud
Multi-language SDK support (Python, Go, TypeScript, Ruby, C#, Java, PHP)
Used in production by large engineering organizations including Uber, DoorDash, and Cloudflare
Automatic retry and state-recovery logic reduces custom failure-handling code
cons: Managed Temporal Cloud plans start at $100/month for Essentials, scaling to custom Enterprise pricing
Requires developers to write workflow code (not a no-code/visual tool)
Additional usage beyond plan quotas is billed per action and per GB-hour of storage

faqs.question: Is Temporal open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Temporal is MIT-licensed and open source, and can be self-hosted for free with community support.

faqs.question: What is Temporal Cloud's starting price?
faqs.answer: The Essentials plan starts at $100/month, including 1M actions and 1GB of active storage.

faqs.question: What programming languages does Temporal support?
faqs.answer: Temporal provides SDKs for Python, Go, TypeScript, Ruby, C#, Java, and PHP.

faqs.question: Does Temporal offer free credits?
faqs.answer: New Temporal Cloud signups can get $1,000 in free credits.

use_cases.title: Long-running business workflows
use_cases.description: Orchestrate multi-step processes like order fulfillment that must survive failures and retries.

use_cases.title: AI agent orchestration
use_cases.description: Coordinate long-running AI agent and pipeline executions with durable state.

use_cases.title: CI/CD pipelines
use_cases.description: Run and track multi-step build and deployment pipelines reliably.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Temporal?
paragraphs: Temporal is a durable execution platform that lets developers write business logic as ordinary code, called Workflows, while the Temporal Service automatically persists state, handles retries, and manages task queues, signals, and timers.
It is open source (MIT-licensed) and can be self-hosted or run as the managed Temporal Cloud service, with SDKs for Python, Go, TypeScript, Ruby, C#, Java, and PHP.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Temporal is for
paragraphs: Temporal is built for engineering teams that need reliable execution of long-running or multi-step processes, such as order fulfillment, CI/CD pipelines, and AI agent orchestration, without writing custom failure-recovery logic.

enrichment.short_description: Process Street is a compliance operations platform for automating workflows, managing policy documents, and maintaining audit-ready records.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Process Street is a compliance operations platform combining document control, AI-powered workflow automation, and audit-ready tracking.

features.title: Docs
features.description: Document management and policy control built for standards like ISO 9001, SOC 2, SOX, and FDA.

features.title: Ops
features.description: AI-powered workflow automation and process orchestration.

features.title: Cora AI agent
features.description: An AI compliance and risk agent that monitors regulations and flags risks.

features.title: Process AI
features.description: Generates workflows automatically from a description.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: Real-time visibility into task and workflow performance.

features.title: Automations
features.description: Reduces manual work by automating repetitive tasks within workflows.

pros: Used by 3,000+ companies and over 1 million users, including Salesforce, Cisco, and Slack
Built-in compliance tooling for ISO 9001, SOC 2, SOX, and FDA standards
14-day free trial on the Pro plan with no credit card required
ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, and GDPR compliant
cons: Enterprise plan pricing is quote-only and requires contacting sales
Startup plan eligibility is restricted to companies under 15 employees and $2M revenue
Exact pricing for Pro and Startup plans is not published on the site

faqs.question: Does Process Street offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pro plan includes a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What compliance standards does Process Street support?
faqs.answer: It supports policy and document control for standards including ISO 9001, SOC 2, SOX, and FDA requirements.

faqs.question: Is there a discounted plan for small businesses?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Startup plan offers a reduced price for businesses with fewer than 15 employees and under $2 million in revenue.

faqs.question: What is Cora?
faqs.answer: Cora is Process Street's AI compliance and risk agent that monitors regulations and flags risks.

use_cases.title: Regulatory compliance tracking
use_cases.description: Maintain audit-ready documentation and policy control for standards like SOC 2 and ISO 9001.

use_cases.title: Operational workflow automation
use_cases.description: Automate recurring operational checklists and processes across departments.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Process Street?
paragraphs: Process Street is a compliance operations platform that combines document control, AI-powered workflow automation, and real-time analytics to help organizations enforce policies and stay audit-ready.
Its Cora AI agent monitors regulatory changes and flags risks, while its Process AI feature can generate workflows from a description.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Process Street is for
paragraphs: It serves operations and compliance teams in regulated sectors such as financial services, healthcare, and manufacturing that need to standardize and document repeatable processes.

enrichment.short_description: Kissflow is an enterprise low-code/no-code platform for building custom business applications, workflows, forms, and portals with AI assistance.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kissflow is a low-code/no-code platform for building business applications, workflows, forms, and portals with AI-assisted app generation.

features.title: Kissflow AI
features.description: Generates complete apps or building blocks from prompts in seconds.

features.title: App Builder
features.description: Low-code/no-code tools for creating custom business applications.

features.title: Workflow Orchestration
features.description: Automates and manages multi-step business processes.

features.title: Form Builder
features.description: Creates custom forms for data capture.

features.title: External Portals
features.description: Client-facing portal solutions for external stakeholders.

features.title: Decision Tables
features.description: Logic-based decision automation within workflows.

features.title: Governance Tools
features.description: Data policies, app monitoring, user monitoring, role-based access, and audit logs.

pros: Combines AI, low-code, and no-code app-building in one platform
Used by 195+ customers including Motorola Solutions, NBC Universal, and University of Michigan
Rated 4.3/5 on G2 and 4.2/5 on Capterra
Available in English, German, French, Spanish, and Italian
No surcharges for AI or API usage on enterprise agreements
cons: Pricing is not published and requires a sales consultation
No self-serve or free plan advertised on the site
Enterprise-only pricing model may not suit smaller teams evaluating cost upfront

faqs.question: What was Kissflow originally called?
faqs.answer: Kissflow began as Orangescape, founded in 2003, before launching the Kissflow SaaS product in 2012.

faqs.question: Is Kissflow pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, pricing is enterprise-focused and requires a consultation with the sales team.

faqs.question: What languages does Kissflow support?
faqs.answer: The platform is available in English, German, French, Spanish, and Italian.

faqs.question: Does Kissflow use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kissflow AI can generate complete apps or app building blocks from a prompt.

use_cases.title: Enterprise app development
use_cases.description: Build custom business applications without a full engineering team.

use_cases.title: Process automation
use_cases.description: Digitize and automate multi-step business workflows.

use_cases.title: External client portals
use_cases.description: Give external stakeholders a controlled interface into internal processes.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Kissflow?
paragraphs: Kissflow is a low-code/no-code platform that lets business users and IT teams build applications, workflows, forms, portals, and boards, with an AI layer that can generate apps or components from a prompt.
The company traces its roots to Orangescape, founded in 2003, before launching Kissflow as a SaaS product in 2012.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Kissflow is for
paragraphs: Kissflow targets enterprise IT leaders, process owners, and citizen developers across industries such as banking, healthcare, retail, and manufacturing who need governed, scalable app development.

enrichment.short_description: Pipefy is a no-code business process orchestration platform that lets teams digitize workflows and build AI agents to automate operations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pipefy is a no-code workflow and business process automation platform with AI agent building tools, offering a free plan for small teams.

features.title: AI Agent Builder
features.description: No-code creation of AI agents that analyze and optimize business processes.

features.title: Workflow Automation
features.description: Design and launch multi-step processes without code.

features.title: Pre-built AI Studios
features.description: Specialized templates for use cases like SRM, CRM, quotation, and onboarding.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects to third-party apps via iPaaS and messaging tools like WhatsApp, Teams, and Slack.

features.title: Custom Portals
features.description: Configurable interfaces for different user audiences.

features.title: Analytics & Monitoring
features.description: Tracks real-time process consumption and AI credit usage.

pros: Free Starter plan available with no credit card required (up to 10 users, 5 processes)
Used by large enterprises including Accenture, BASF, IBM, and Kraft Heinz
Supports English and Portuguese
SMB plan offers up to 90% discount for companies with 11-200 employees
Includes electronic signature and API access on the Business plan
cons: Business and Enterprise plan pricing is quote-only, not published
Free Starter plan is capped at 10 users, 5 processes, and 50 cards/month
Additional AI credits beyond the included 1,000 per plan require an add-on purchase

faqs.question: Does Pipefy have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan is free for up to 10 users, 5 processes, and 50 cards per month, with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What languages does Pipefy support?
faqs.answer: Pipefy is available in English and Portuguese (PT-BR).

faqs.question: Does Pipefy include AI features on the free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pipefy AI and 1,000 single-use AI credits are included on all plans, including Starter.

faqs.question: Is there a discount for small businesses?
faqs.answer: Yes, an SMB Special Plan offers up to 90% discount for companies with 11-200 employees.

use_cases.title: Business process digitization
use_cases.description: Replace spreadsheets and email with structured, trackable workflows.

use_cases.title: AI agent-driven automation
use_cases.description: Build AI agents to handle repetitive process steps like claims validation.

use_cases.title: Procurement and vendor management
use_cases.description: Use pre-built SRM and P2P studios to manage supplier processes.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pipefy?
paragraphs: Pipefy is a no-code business process orchestration platform that lets teams digitize workflows and build AI agents to analyze, optimize, and evolve their processes.
It offers pre-built AI Studios for specific functions like procurement, CRM, and onboarding, plus a free Starter plan for smaller teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pipefy is for
paragraphs: Pipefy serves enterprise teams across financial services, insurance, manufacturing, HR, legal, and procurement that need to standardize and automate cross-functional processes.

enrichment.short_description: Tallyfy is an AI-native workflow platform for documenting repeatable processes and routing steps to people, AI agents, or connected apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tallyfy is a workflow and process management platform that lets teams document processes once and assign steps to people or AI agents.

features.title: Process Documentation
features.description: Create workflows with AI assistance and document repeatable processes once.

features.title: Multi-actor Execution
features.description: Route steps to people, AI agents, or connected apps.

features.title: Real-time Tracking
features.description: Live status visibility for all tasks and processes.

features.title: Conditional Logic
features.description: Branch processes based on conditions.

features.title: Client-facing Access
features.description: External participants can complete steps without logging in.

features.title: AI Integration
features.description: MCP server plus Claude and ChatGPT integrations for agent-based execution.

pros: Transparent published pricing: $30/month or $300/year per Full Seat, $10/month or $100/year per Light Seat
All plans include free SSO, unlimited guests, and a 14-day trial
SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, and EU-U.S. DPF certified
Integrates with Microsoft Entra ID, Google Workspace, Okta, and OneLogin
Available on web, iOS, and Android
cons: Annual billing required for the discounted rate; monthly billing costs more per seat
Analytics (Data Feed) is a paid add-on at +$20/seat/year
Minimum commitment of 1 seat required

faqs.question: How much does Tallyfy cost?
faqs.answer: A Full Seat costs $300/year ($30/month), and a Light Seat costs $100/year ($10/month).

faqs.question: Does Tallyfy offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans include a 14-day trial period.

faqs.question: When was Tallyfy founded?
faqs.answer: Tallyfy was founded in 2014.

faqs.question: What platforms does Tallyfy support?
faqs.answer: Tallyfy is available on web, iOS, and Android.

faqs.question: Does Tallyfy integrate with AI tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers an MCP server and integrations with Claude and ChatGPT.

use_cases.title: Process standardization
use_cases.description: Document a process once and reuse it across the organization.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted task routing
use_cases.description: Assign workflow steps to AI agents alongside human team members.

use_cases.title: Client onboarding
use_cases.description: Let external clients complete process steps without needing an account.

features: Access to Administrator, Standard, and Light roles

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tallyfy?
paragraphs: Tallyfy, founded in 2014, is an AI-native workflow platform that lets organizations document a process once and then assign each step to people, AI agents, or connected apps while tracking execution in real time.
It integrates with identity providers like Microsoft Entra ID and Google Workspace, and offers an MCP server plus Claude and ChatGPT integrations for AI-driven task execution.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tallyfy is for
paragraphs: Tallyfy targets mid-size companies that need to scale repeatable operations without a dedicated internal AI or automation team.

enrichment.short_description: Ninox is an AI-assisted low-code database platform that lets business teams build custom apps for managing records, documents, and workflows.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Ninox is an AI-native low-code database platform for building custom business apps, with a free plan and paid tiers from €25/user/month.

features.title: AI-assisted app building
features.description: Users describe what they need and the platform generates the application.

features.title: Workplace collaboration
features.description: Integrates directly with Gmail and Outlook.

features.title: Document management
features.description: Generates contracts, invoices, and reports from app data.

features.title: Workflow orchestration
features.description: Automated triggers and custom business logic.

features.title: Multiple views
features.description: Table, form, chart, kanban, maps, and calendar views of the same data.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Available starting on the Team plan.

pros: Free plan available indefinitely with no credit card required, for up to 5 users
Founded in 2014 and headquartered in Berlin, Germany
ISO 27001, GDPR, and ISO 9001 compliant with German-hosted data centers
Available in English and German
Gmail and Outlook integration built in
cons: Free plan is capped at 5,000 records, 1,000 API calls/month, and 100 MB storage
Advanced AI prompts are only available on the Business plan and above
Enterprise plan pricing is custom and requires contacting sales

faqs.question: Does Ninox have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan supports up to 5 users, 5,000 records, and 100 MB storage indefinitely, with no credit card required.

faqs.question: When was Ninox founded?
faqs.answer: Ninox was founded in 2014 by Frank Böhmer.

faqs.question: Where is Ninox headquartered?
faqs.answer: Ninox is headquartered in Berlin, Germany.

faqs.question: How much does Ninox's paid plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Team plan starts at €25 per user/month, billed annually.

use_cases.title: Custom business database apps
use_cases.description: Build apps for managing records, documents, and workflows without traditional coding.

use_cases.title: Document generation
use_cases.description: Automatically generate contracts, invoices, and reports from app data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Ninox?
paragraphs: Ninox is a low-code database platform, founded in 2014 in Berlin, that lets business teams build custom apps by describing what they need in natural language.
It offers table, form, chart, kanban, map, and calendar views over the same underlying data, plus built-in Gmail and Outlook integration.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Ninox is for
paragraphs: Ninox is aimed at business teams that need custom software for managing records and documents but don't have dedicated development resources.

enrichment.short_description: Stacker is an AI-powered no-code platform that builds internal business applications, CRMs, and portals from natural-language descriptions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Stacker is an AI-powered no-code app builder that turns natural-language requests into internal tools, CRMs, and client portals.

features.title: AI app builder
features.description: Users describe what they need in natural language and Stacker generates a functional application.

features.title: Layout and feature editing via AI
features.description: The AI can redesign layouts, add features, and restructure entire applications on request.

features.title: Custom code components
features.description: AI-generated custom code can extend the platform beyond standard no-code capabilities.

features.title: Customer portals
features.description: White-labeled, unlimited customer portals with custom domains.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects to over 2,000 third-party tools.

features.title: Scheduled automations
features.description: Supports scheduled tasks and automations within built applications.

pros: Free trial with $100 in credits, no credit card required
No per-seat charges on the Standard plan
Access to 2,000+ integrations and unlimited customer portals
AI builder can restructure entire apps, not just generate templates
cons: Standard plan pricing is credit-based, which can be harder to predict than flat per-seat pricing
Enterprise plan pricing is quote-only
Higher credit tiers scale up to $2,000/month for heavy usage

faqs.question: How much does Stacker cost?
faqs.answer: The Standard plan starts at $50/month for 250 credits, with higher credit tiers available up to $2,000/month.

faqs.question: Does Stacker offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, new users get $100 in credits with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Stacker charge per user?
faqs.answer: No, Stacker states there are no per-seat charges on its Standard plan.

faqs.question: What is Stacker's AI builder?
faqs.answer: Stacker's AI builder lets users describe what they need in natural language and generates a functional no-code application, including redesigning layouts and adding features on request.

use_cases.title: Internal tool building
use_cases.description: Generate internal business applications from a natural-language description instead of manual no-code configuration.

use_cases.title: Client portals
use_cases.description: Build white-labeled, unlimited customer-facing portals with custom domains.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Stacker?
paragraphs: Stacker is a no-code application platform where users describe the app they need in natural language and its AI builder generates a functional tool, which can then be edited visually.
Beneath the AI layer is a traditional no-code platform designed to support business-critical applications, with over 2,000 integrations and white-labeled customer portals.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Stacker is for
paragraphs: Stacker is designed for non-technical business users who need custom internal tools, CRMs, or client portals without hiring developers.

enrichment.short_description: Noloco is a no-code platform for building custom CRMs, project trackers, client portals, and internal apps connected to existing data sources.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Noloco is a no-code platform for building CRMs, client portals, and internal apps, with a free plan and paid tiers starting at $99/month.

features.title: Interface Builder
features.description: Point-and-click app creation without coding.

features.title: Connected Data (Noloco Tables)
features.description: Real-time data management with sync capabilities to external sources.

features.title: Nola AI Assistant
features.description: Builds and customizes apps from natural-language prompts.

features.title: Workflows
features.description: Custom automations to reduce manual work.

features.title: Granular permissions
features.description: Access control at table, page, record, and field levels.

features.title: Public access sharing
features.description: Share specific app sections publicly while keeping other areas private.

pros: Free plan with 3 team seats, 7 client seats, and 2,000 rows, no credit card required
14-day free trial included on all paid plans
40+ integrations including Airtable, Google Sheets, Slack, HubSpot, and Stripe
50% discount for non-profits on any paid plan
Used by 1,000+ professional services firms
cons: Pro plan jumps to $99/month (or $149/month if paying monthly instead of annually)
Extra team/client seats and rows beyond plan limits cost additional per-unit fees
Enterprise plan pricing requires a custom quote

faqs.question: Does Noloco have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 3 team seats, 7 client seats, and 2,000 rows with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Noloco's Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan is $99/month billed monthly, or $149/month effectively when billed annually with a 33% discount shown on the site.

faqs.question: What integrations does Noloco support?
faqs.answer: Noloco offers 40+ integrations including Airtable, Google Sheets, Zapier, Slack, HubSpot, Stripe, and QuickBooks.

faqs.question: What is Nola?
faqs.answer: Nola is Noloco's AI assistant that builds and customizes apps from natural-language prompts.

use_cases.title: Client portals
use_cases.description: Build white-labeled portals for clients to access project data.

use_cases.title: Internal CRMs and trackers
use_cases.description: Create custom CRMs and project trackers connected to existing data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Noloco?
paragraphs: Noloco is a no-code platform that lets teams build custom CRMs, project trackers, client portals, and dashboards connected to existing data sources like Airtable and Google Sheets.
Its Nola AI assistant can build and customize apps from natural-language prompts, and a free plan is available alongside paid tiers starting at $99/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Noloco is for
paragraphs: Noloco targets professional services firms, agencies, and small-to-medium businesses that want custom internal systems without hiring developers.

enrichment.short_description: WeWeb is a no-code visual development platform that generates web applications from AI prompts and exports production-ready Vue.js code.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: WeWeb is a no-code platform combining AI app generation with a visual editor, exporting production-ready Vue.js code for self-hosting.

features.title: AI Assistant
features.description: Generates full web apps, pages, databases, and logic from text descriptions.

features.title: Visual Editor
features.description: Drag-and-drop interface for editing without code.

features.title: Code Export
features.description: Outputs production-ready Vue.js Single Page Applications for self-hosting.

features.title: Backend Builder
features.description: Tools for creating databases and application logic.

features.title: Figma import
features.description: Imports designs directly from Figma.

features.title: Database integrations
features.description: Connects to Airtable, Xano, and Supabase.

pros: Founded in 2019 and headquartered in Paris, France
Full code ownership: apps can be exported and self-hosted
No per-app-user charges according to the site
SOC2, HIPAA, and GDPR compliance support
Used by large companies including PwC, L'Oreal, and Carrefour
cons: Detailed dollar pricing is not consistently published on the main site (pricing page is a separate, frequently-updated subdomain)
Requires a paid Seat plan (Essential or above) before a Hosting plan can be added
Visual/no-code editor still assumes some technical familiarity for backend logic

faqs.question: Does WeWeb offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, WeWeb's documentation lists Free tiers among both its Seat plans and Hosting plans.

faqs.question: Can I export code from WeWeb?
faqs.answer: Yes, WeWeb outputs production-ready Vue.js Single Page Applications that can be self-hosted.

faqs.question: When was WeWeb founded?
faqs.answer: WeWeb was founded in 2019.

faqs.question: What databases does WeWeb connect to?
faqs.answer: WeWeb integrates with Airtable, Xano, and Supabase, among other data sources.

use_cases.title: MVP and app building
use_cases.description: Generate a working web app from an AI prompt and refine it visually.

use_cases.title: Internal tools and client apps
use_cases.description: Build internal or client-facing web apps with exportable, self-hostable code.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is WeWeb?
paragraphs: WeWeb, founded in 2019 in Paris, is a no-code visual development platform that can generate full web applications from AI prompts and lets users refine them with a drag-and-drop editor.
Unlike many no-code tools, WeWeb exports production-ready Vue.js code that can be self-hosted, and it connects to backends like Airtable, Xano, and Supabase.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who WeWeb is for
paragraphs: WeWeb serves founders building MVPs as well as larger organizations and agencies that need custom web apps with code ownership and self-hosting options.

name: Bildr
skip_reason: Bildr's official website (bildr.com) now shows only a company shutdown/farewell message stating the product is discontinued ("That world is arriving, and even though it won't be with Bildr..."). No product, pricing, or feature content remains, so no accurate current listing can be produced.
